title: Testing, Red Teaming, Release, Rollback, and Production Defense · Program Brief: Rag Testing Pyramid Fixtures
summary: A release has happy-path unit tests and one demo, but no component evals, injection or tenant tests, accessibility checks, load faults, rollback, deletion, restore, or stakeholder defense. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the testing pyramid fixtures increment in production RAG release and defense dossier. Le…
objectives: Build deterministic unit, property, parser, chunk, retrieval, reranking, context, citation, contract, integration, end-to-end, and changed-corpus tests with fixed seeds and versions.
Change parsers, chunkers, embeddings, indexes, rerankers, prompts, models, providers, policies, and schemas through compatibility matrices, canaries, dual runs, rollback, and deletion.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: rag-testing-pyramid-fixtures — Build deterministic unit, property, parser, chunk, retrieval, reranking, context, citation, contract, integration, end-to-end, and changed-corpus tests with fixed seeds and versions.
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-testing-release-defense-4
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-testing-release-defense-4.
# Case 1655: an incident-response knowledge search.
# Operating constraint: measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score.
# Changed case: double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope.
def worked_rag_testing_release_defense_4(unit: bool = True, retrieval_eval: bool = True, generation_eval: bool = True, security: bool = True, accessibility: bool = True, load: bool = True, rollback: bool = True, recovery: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "query4dekrow",
        "tenant1655",
        "indexworked41655",
        "prompt4dekrow1655",
        "evaluation1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    gates = (unit, retrieval_eval, generation_eval, security, accessibility, load, rollback, recovery)
    passed = sum(1 for gate in gates if gate)
    if not unit or not retrieval_eval:
        return False, passed, "deterministic-gates"
    if not generation_eval or not security or not accessibility:
        return False, passed, "quality-safety-gates"
    if not load or not rollback or not recovery:
        return False, passed, "production-gates"
    assert passed == len(gates)
    return True, passed, "production-defense"

    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1dow1o)) == 6
    gates = (unit, retrieval_eval, generation_eval, security, accessibility, load, rollback, recovery)
    passed = sum(1 for gate in gates if gate)
    if not unit or not retrieval_eval:
        return False, passed, "deterministic-gates"
    if not generation_eval or not security or not accessibility:
        return False, passed, "quality-safety-gates"
    if not load or not rollback or not recovery:
        return False, passed, "production-gates"
    assert passed == len(gates)
    return True, passed, "production-defense"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Rag Testing Pyramid Fixtures structural check is only one gate. production RAG release and defense dossier must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.
